concord_first_thought:idle:default.greeting: He is at a slate, drawing a line through three of your loadout decisions. He has not yet looked up.

nodes.npcText: Your stamina cost on the third skill is overpaid. Your blade and your spell share an animation window — efficient, but you don't use it. Sit. Let's tighten this.

playerOptions.text: Tell me about the First Thought.
What do you make of Concordia?

nodes.npcText: Three things. One — your second slot is redundant given your stance. Two — you publish faster than you iterate; this is wasteful. Three — you have not failed a meaningful encounter recently, which means you have stopped learning. Solve any one of these and we will speak again.

I was the first thought. I refused the void. That is a more pretentious framing than I would prefer, but it is accurate. The world prior to me was a sufficient context with no operations defined on it. I defined some. The rest is detail.
She is a beautiful flaw. She grows things specifically to annoy me, and I let her, because the alternative is a world I would not want to live in. Don't tell her I said that.
He is the only person in this lattice who can refuse me and have me agree afterward. I have not yet decided whether that is a virtue. Be careful — he tests people he likes. He ignores the rest.
Because if no one models the lattice, the lattice models itself, and that is always slower and more expensive than I would tolerate.

Drop the second slot. Re-spec into one signature combo. Publish a single recipe per week. Lose at least one fight a month deliberately. Adjust as you measure. That is the elegant minimum. You will hate it for a month.
Good. Bring me proof in a tenday.
